subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text:
Let \(A_1,A_2,\ldots,A_k\) be subsets of \([n]\) such that \(A_i\cap A_j\ne\varnothing\), for all \(i,j\). Show that
\[
k\le 2^{n-1}.
\]
Is this inequality best possible?

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: How many pairs \((A,B)\) of subsets of \([n]\) are there such that \(A\cap B=\varnothing\)?

problems.text: Use inclusion–exclusion to determine the number of monic polynomials of degree \(n\) with no roots in \(\mathbb{Z}_p[x]\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that the number of partitions of \(n\) into at most \(k\) parts equals the number of partitions of \(n\) into parts of size at most \(k\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that the number of partitions of \(n\) into odd parts is equal to the number of partitions of \(n\) into unequal parts. (Hint: use generating functions).

problems.text:
Prove that the number of \(k\)-dimensional subspaces of the \(n\) dimensional vector space over \(\mathbb{F}_q\) is
\[
\binom{n}{k}_q=\frac{(q^n-1)\cdots(q^{n-k+1}-1)}{(q^k-1)\cdots(q-1)}.
\]
Prove that every regular bipartite graph has a perfect matching. Show that this is not true in general for regular graphs of even order.
Prove that in a binary self dual code, either all the vectors have weight divisible by 4 or half have even weight not divisible by 4 and half have weight divisible by 4.
Let \(H\) be a parity check matrix for a binary \((n,k,d)\)-code \(C\) with \(n\ge 4\). If the columns of \(H\) are distinct and all have odd weight, then \(d\ge 4\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show the nonexistence of a difference set with parameters \((31,10,3)\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show the nonexistence of a symmetric design with parameters \((29,8,2)\). (Hint: consider the appropriate equation modulo 3)

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Show the nonexistence of a Steiner system \(S(3,6,11)\).

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Find a \((13,4,1)\) difference set \(D\). The set of translates of \(D\) form the blocks of a design. Find the parameters \(b,v,k,r,\lambda\).

problems.text: Let \(A\) be an affine plane of order \(n\). Write \(B_1\sim B_2\) if lines \(B_1\) and \(B_2\) are the same or have no points in common. Show that \(\sim\) is an equivalence relation. Show that there exists a projective plane \(\pi\) such that \(A\) is obtained from \(\pi\) by deleting one line and all the points on that line.
